Class DefaultAppContext

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.context.DefaultAppContext
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable, AppContext
public class DefaultAppContext extends Object implements AppContext
Default implementation of AppContext. Uses insertion order with override-by-prepend semantics:
  • register(Class, Object) → appends
  • registerOverride(Class, Object) → prepends
  • get(Class) → returns first match

    registerOverride(Class<T> type, T instance)
    Registers a new component under the given type, guaranteeing that AppContext.get(Class) returns that component instance after call to this method.

  • Constructor Details

    • DefaultAppContext

      public DefaultAppContext()

  • Method Details

    • get

      public <T> T get(@NonNull @NonNull Class<T> type)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Gets a bean instance by its type (usually an interface type). There's no guarantee to which bean will be returned if multiple beans were registered under that type.
      Specified by:
      get in interface AppContext
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the bean
      Parameters:
      type - the type that was used to register the bean
      Returns:
      the bean instance
      See Also:
      • AppContext.getAll(Class)

    • getAll

      public <T> Collection<T> getAll(@NonNull @NonNull Class<T> type)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Gets all registered beans by the given type.
      Specified by:
      getAll in interface AppContext
      Type Parameters:
      T - the common supertype of the beans
      Parameters:
      type - the type
      Returns:
      all beans registered under that type
      See Also:
      • AppContext.get(Class)

    • count

      public int count(@NonNull @NonNull Class<?> type)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Returns the count of beans registered under the given type.

      This method doesn't initialize lazy beans, so it can be used instead of getAll(type).size() to avoid initialization when only the count of them is needed.

      Specified by:
      count in interface AppContext
      Parameters:
      type - the type
      Returns:
      the count of beans registered under the given type

    • has

      public boolean has(Class<?> type)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Determines whether there is at least one bean registered under the given type. Returns true if so and false otherwise.

      This method doesn't initialize lazy beans, so it can be used instead of !getAll(type).isEmpty() to avoid initialization when only needed to check presence.

      Implementations may choose to return false instead of throwing a NullPointerException when the given type is null.

      Specified by:
      has in interface AppContext
      Parameters:
      type - the type
      Returns:
      whether any beans are registered under the given type

    • register

      public <T> DefaultAppContext register(Class<T> type, T instance)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Registers a new component under the given type. Multiple components can be registered under the same type.
      Specified by:
      register in interface AppContext
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      instance - the component instance to register
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      See Also:
      • AppContext.registerLazy(Class, Function)
      • AppContext.registerOverride(Class, Object)

    • registerOverride

      public <T> DefaultAppContext registerOverride(Class<T> type, T instance)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Registers a new component under the given type, guaranteeing that AppContext.get(Class) returns that component instance after call to this method.
      Specified by:
      registerOverride in interface AppContext
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      instance - the component instance to register, overriding other instances with the same type
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      See Also:
      • AppContext.registerLazy(Class, Function)

    • registerLazy

      public <T> DefaultAppContext registerLazy(Class<T> type, Function<? super AppContext, ? extends T> factory)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Registers a lazy-initialized component under the given type. Lazy components are not initialized until they're requested with AppContext.get(Class) or similar methods.

      This is useful for registering heavy beans that might never be used, or beans that do some irreversible changes once created.

      Specified by:
      registerLazy in interface AppContext
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      factory - a factory function that accepts this context and produces a new component instance
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      See Also:
      • AppContext.registerLazy(Class, Supplier)

    • registerLazy

      public <T> DefaultAppContext registerLazy(Class<T> type, Supplier<? extends T> factory)
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Registers a lazy-initialized component under the given type. Lazy components are not initialized until they're requested with AppContext.get(Class) or similar methods.

      This is useful for registering heavy beans that might never be used, or beans that do some irreversible changes once created.

      This method is a version of AppContext.registerLazy(Class, Function) where the factory function does not accept arguments.

      Specified by:
      registerLazy in interface AppContext
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the component
      Parameters:
      type - the type (usually an interface type implemented by the component)
      factory - a factory function that produces a new component instance
      Returns:
      this context, for chaining calls
      See Also:
      • AppContext.registerLazy(Class, Function)

    • close

      public void close() throws Exception
      Description copied from interface: AppContext
      Closes this context and releases beans that implement AutoCloseable. The context cannot be used after calling this method. Calling this method more than once does not have any effect.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AppContext
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable

    • createWithDefaults

      public static DefaultAppContext createWithDefaults()
      Creates a new application context with default services pre-registered.
      Returns:
      a new context

    • createHeadless

      public static DefaultAppContext createHeadless()
      Creates a new headless application context (without audio or graphics services).
      Returns:
      a new context
